name: Bugis
notes: Buginese (Basa Ugi) — South Sulawesi (Austronesian) language of Indonesia. Traditionally written in the Lontara script, a Brahmic abugida (each consonant letter carries an inherent /a/ modified by vowel diacritics; final consonants and geminates are systematically not written), and today also in the Latin script. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED for either script: the Lontara defective encoding of codas and gemination makes a reliable grapheme→IPA table a research task. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Lontara script (Unicode block Buginese) or in the Latin orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode bugi1244), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode bugi1244.
